issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions update the visual state and body classes but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents screen reader users from knowing if the navigation menu is currently deployed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1640: `function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }` Line 1665: `function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher initialization function applies the resolved theme but does not set the initial aria-pressed state on the toggle buttons. The state is only updated after the first manual click.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5135: `function init() { ... _current = _load(); const resolved = _resolve(_current); _apply(resolved); qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => { setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') === _current)); }); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the result count badge is updated visually, but this change is not announced to screen readers. Users may be unaware that the number of products in the grid has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2375: `function _updateCountBadge(count) { if (!_countBadge) return; const msg = `${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`; _countBadge.textContent = String(count); _countBadge.setAttribute('aria-label', msg); liveRegion.announce(msg); }`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function associates a field with an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set the aria-invalid attribute. Assistive technologies will not communicate that the field is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: `function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { if (!isElement(field) || !isElement(errorEl)) return; setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); }`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the error description but does not remove or reset the aria-invalid attribute. A field previously marked invalid will continue to be announced as invalid even after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1555: `function markValid(field, errorEl) { if (!isElement(field)) return; removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID); ... }`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger is not initialized with an aria-expanded attribute. Screen readers will not announce the initial collapsed state of the menu when the page loads.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1715: `function init() { ... _initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Breadcrumb link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The breadcrumb processing function identifies the last item in the list but fails to apply aria-current="page" to the link. Users of assistive technology cannot distinguish the current page from other links in the breadcrumb trail.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2015: `// Mark the last item as the current page. if (lastLink) lastLink.setAttribute('aria-current', 'page');`

issuesFound.title: Toast notification missing pause/stop mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications are automatically dismissed after a short duration (3.5s to 6s) without a way for the user to pause or extend the timer. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 for users who need more time to read the message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145: `if (dur > 0) { timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur); on(toast, 'mouseenter focusin', () => { if (timerId) { clearTimeout(timerId); timerId = null; } }); }`

issuesFound.title: Modal/Drawer missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open function locks the scroll and shows the drawer but fails to activate the focus trap. Keyboard users can still navigate to elements behind the off-canvas menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1640: `function open(announceToSR) { ... _lockScroll(); if (_trap) _trap.activate(); emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer }); }`

issuesFound.title: Progress bar missing required ARIA attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step wizard progress bar is a div that lacks the progressbar role and the mandatory aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax attributes. It is not identifiable as a progress indicator by assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4765: `if (_progressBar) { setAttr(_progressBar, ARIA.ROLE, 'progressbar'); setAttr(_progressBar, ARIA.VALUEMIN, '0'); setAttr(_progressBar, ARIA.VALUEMAX, '100'); }`

issuesFound.title: Interactive element missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not linked to the navigation drawer it controls via the aria-controls attribute, making the relationship between the button and the menu unclear to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1715: `function init() { ... _drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav); if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer')); ... }`

fpTitles: Toast notification missing pause/stop mechanism
Progress bar missing required ARIA attributes

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle() function changes the visibility of the navigation drawer but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.open() function, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); In the mobileNav.close() function, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions show/hide dropdown panels but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. This breaks accessibility for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); In _closeInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch (button) changes state but does not update aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot perceive the current state of the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing.setPeriod, after updating _pricingState.period, add: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons (data-filter-value) change active state but only update CSS classes, not aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot tell which tab is selected.
issuesFound.fix: In _syncTabAriaStates, for each button, add: setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', isActive ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and the count badge changes, but there is no live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed of the content change.
issuesFound.fix: In productFilter.applyFilters, after _updateCountBadge, add: liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${totalMatches} products`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria-invalid on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks invalid fields with aria-invalid="true" but when a field becomes valid, it only removes aria-describedby and does not set aria-invalid="false". This leaves an incorrect invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, when result.valid is true, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: On page load, the mobile navigation drawer is closed, but the trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set initially. Screen readers may not know the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, after setting _trigger, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: On page load, dropdown panels are hidden, but the trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded="false" set initially. Screen readers may not know the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.init, after registering each instance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector removes the outline (outline: none) and does not provide any alternative visible focus style such as a box‑shadow or colored border. Keyboard‑only users lose the visual cue that an element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: :focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The selector removes the outline for focus states that are not focus‑visible, again leaving no visible focus cue for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 305: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons lose their outline on :focus-visible and no alternative styling is supplied, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which button is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: .btn:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes its outline on focus‑visible without providing another visible focus style, breaking keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter‑tab buttons strip their outline on focus‑visible and do not add any alternative visual cue, preventing users from tracking focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 420: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle buttons lose their outline on focus‑visible with no replacement style, hindering keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 460: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove their outline on focus‑visible and provide no alternative visual focus indicator, making it difficult for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 500: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: -3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets a min‑height of 20 px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 recommended 44 × 44 px touch target, making it hard for users with motor impairments to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 260: .btn-sm { min-height: 32px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon‑only buttons define min‑width and min‑height of 20 px, which is smaller than the required 44 px touch target, reducing tap accuracy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 270: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: auto; }

issuesFound.title: Visually hidden utility removes content from AT (.sr-only)
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width/height: auto and does not apply clipping, which means the element remains visible to sighted users and is not reliably hidden from assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 210: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Visually hidden utility removes content from AT (.not-sr-only)
issuesFound.explanation: The .not-sr-only class mirrors the incorrect .sr-only implementation, also failing to hide content from assistive technologies while removing it visually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 220: .not-sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image animates its transform on hover, but there is no reduced‑motion media query that disables or simplifies this animation, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img, .hero-image img:hover { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of the hero image applies a transform animation without a reduced‑motion guard, causing unwanted motion for users who have requested reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 570: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links, and no other visual cue (border, background, font‑weight) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 150: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets `word-spacing: -0.2em;`, which compresses spacing between words and can reduce readability, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 120: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The element used as the mobile‑nav trigger never receives an aria‑controls attribute linking it to the navigation drawer, so screen readers cannot associate the button with the region it shows/hides.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle buttons lack aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons (`[data-period]`) are styled as a toggle group but never receive aria‑pressed updates, leaving AT users without feedback about which billing period is active.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not set aria‑expanded to true when a panel opens
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.open(item)`, the panel is shown with animation but the trigger’s aria‑expanded attribute is never switched to "true". Consequently, screen‑reader users cannot tell which FAQ item is expanded.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function calls `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl)` when validation fails, but `markInvalid` only adds a descriptive error element and never sets the required aria‑invalid attribute. AT therefore cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not update `aria-expanded` on their trigger buttons.
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` in the `dropdownMenu` module add/remove CSS classes and hide/show panels, but never set `aria-expanded` on the associated trigger element, breaking the disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close functions do not update `aria-expanded` on the question buttons.
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open()` and `faqAccordion.close()` animate the answer panel but never toggle `aria-expanded` on the trigger element, so assistive technologies cannot convey the open state.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update `aria-pressed` when toggled.
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing._syncToggleState()` adds/removes the visual `active` class on period buttons but never updates `aria-pressed`, leaving the button’s pressed state invisible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing‑toggle switch does not update `aria-pressed`.
issuesFound.explanation: The element with `data-billing-toggle` acts as a switch (role `switch`) but its `aria-pressed` state is never changed when the user clicks it, violating the ARIA switch pattern.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter category tabs do not update `aria-pressed` when selected.
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter._syncTabAriaStates()` toggles a visual `active` class on filter buttons but never updates `aria-pressed`, so the selected category is not announced to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1260: `.feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible` rule sets `outline: none` without a visible replacement, removing focus visibility for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1610: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `.pricing-toggle button:focus-visible` rule sets `outline: none` without providing a visible focus indicator, violating keyboard accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1420: `.pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible` rule sets `outline: none` without a visible replacement, removing focus visibility for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1900: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` rule sets `min-height: 20px`, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size recommended by WCAG 2.5.8 (Target Size). This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to interact with small buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-icon` rule sets `min-width: 20px` and `min-height: 20px`, which are below the 24px minimum touch target size recommended by WCAG 2.5.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 630: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button
issuesFound.explanation: The `#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button` rule sets `min-height: 28px`, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. While 28px meets the minimum, it does not meet the recommended size for better usability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 690: `#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: The `[id$="-submenu"] li a` rule sets `min-height: 28px`, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 710: `[id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: The `.filter-tabs button` rule sets `min-height: 28px`, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1070: `.filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .product-body .btn-secondary
issuesFound.explanation: The `.product-body .btn-secondary` rule sets `min-height: 28px`, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1180: `.product-body .btn-secondary { min-height: 44px; font-size: var(--font-size-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .pricing-card .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The `.pricing-card .btn` rule sets no explicit `min-height`, but inherits from `.btn` (32px). While 32px meets the minimum, it does not meet the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1490: `.pricing-card .btn { min-height: 44px; width: 100%; justify-content: center; }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class incorrectly uses `position: static` and `clip: auto`, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as content intended for screen readers may become visible or misplaced.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` and `.hero-image img:hover` rules apply transform animations without a corresponding `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow the motion. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: The `.feature-card:hover` rule applies a `transform` animation without a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable the motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: The `.product-card:hover` rule applies a `transform` animation without a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable the motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: The `.pricing-card:hover` rule applies a `transform` animation without a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable the motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured
issuesFound.explanation: The `.pricing-card--featured:hover` rule applies a `transform` animation without a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable the motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: The `.stat-item:hover` rule applies a `transform` animation without a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable the motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision by making word separation less clear.
issuesFound.fix: Line 220: `body { word-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `letter-spacing: -0.05em`, which can reduce readability for users with cognitive or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 220: `body { letter-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule removes underlines from links without providing an alternative visual differentiator (e.g., border-bottom, background highlight, or font-weight increase on hover/focus). This violates WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color) as links rely solely on color to be distinguished from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 240: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`
    Alternatively, if underlines are undesirable, add:
    `a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: underline; }` or `a { border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; }`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `toggle` function in `mobileNav` toggles the CSS class `is-open` on the drawer but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state change, impacting users who rely on assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10824: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
    Line 10830: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-pressed on toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in `pricing` toggles the billing period but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen readers cannot announce the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11810: `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, String(next === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` change the navigation menu state but do not announce the change to screen readers via a live region. Users who rely on assistive technologies may not be aware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10824: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
    Line 10830: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in `inlineValidator` identifies validation errors but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input element. This prevents screen readers from announcing the error state, violating WCAG 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13050: `field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`
    Line 13060: `field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `mobileNav` does not initialise the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`) to reflect the initial closed state of the navigation menu. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as the initial state is not conveyed to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10810: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `mobileNav` is triggered by a click event on the `_trigger` element, but there is no corresponding keyboard event handler (e.g., `keydown` for Enter/Space) to ensure keyboard operability. This violates WCAG 2.1.1.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content injected without ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `show` function in `toastManager` injects dynamic toast notifications into the DOM using `innerHTML` but does not use an ARIA live region to announce these changes to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3, as users may not be aware of the new notifications.

issuesFound.title: aria-describedby reference to non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mount` function in `suggestionListRenderer` sets `aria-describedby` on the search input to reference a status element (`_statusEl`) that may not exist in the DOM at the time of assignment. This creates a broken ARIA reference, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: aria-controls reference to non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mount` function in `suggestionListRenderer` sets `aria-controls` on the search input to reference a listbox (`_listEl`) that may not have an ID at the time of assignment. This creates a broken ARIA reference, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
aria-describedby reference to non-existent element

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` toggle the CSS class `is-open` on the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state change, leaving keyboard and assistive technology users unaware of the navigation menu's open/closed status.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10230: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
    Line 10236: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` change the navigation state but do not announce this change to screen readers via a live region. Users who rely on screen readers are not informed when the navigation menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10230: After opening, add:
```
liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');
```
    Line 10236: After closing, add:
```
liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');
```

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `mobileNav` does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button (`_trigger`) when the navigation menu is closed on page load. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the initial state is not programmatically exposed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10250: After `_trigger` is assigned, add:
```
setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in `inlineValidator` updates the visual error state (adds/removes CSS classes and error messages) but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification) as the error state is not programmatically exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12250: In the invalid branch, add:
```
setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');
```
    In the valid branch, add:
```
setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in `inlineValidator` removes the error message and CSS classes when a field becomes valid but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the `aria-invalid` attribute. This can leave the field in an inconsistent state where it is visually valid but still marked as invalid to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onTriggerClick` function in `mobileNav` handles click events on the navigation toggle button but does not provide a corresponding keyboard handler for `keydown` events (specifically Enter and Space). This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as the button cannot be activated via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute for personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The email input field in the newsletter form (`_emailEl`) does not have an `autocomplete` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose) as browsers and password managers cannot automatically fill in the user's email address.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12650: After `_emailEl` is assigned, add:
```
setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');
```

issuesFound.title: Missing fieldset and legend for grouped form controls
issuesFound.explanation: The consent checkbox in the contact form (`[name="consent"]`) is not wrapped in a `<fieldset>` with a `<legend>`. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) as the relationship between the checkbox and its group label is not programmatically exposed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12750: In the HTML, wrap the consent checkbox and its label in a fieldset:
```
<fieldset>
  <legend>Privacy consent</legend>
  <label>
    <input type="checkbox" name="consent" required>
    I agree to the privacy policy
  </label>
</fieldset>
```

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
Missing autocomplete attribute for personal data input
Missing fieldset and legend for grouped form controls

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the CSS class `is-open` on the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware of the navigation state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5621: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
    Line 5630: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`
    Line 5635: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `close(false);`
    Line 5640: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `open();`
    Line 5645: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, _open ? 'true' : 'false');` after `_open = !_open;`
    Line 5650: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `close(false);`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `mobileNav` module does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the trigger button when the navigation drawer is closed on page load. Screen readers may announce the button as expanded by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5660: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after the trigger element is assigned.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module change the navigation state but do not announce the change to screen readers via a live region. Users who rely on screen readers are unaware of the navigation state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5621: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`
    Line 5630: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function returns a validation result but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element when validation fails. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module sets `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` on valid fields. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid after correction.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module's trigger button (`_trigger`) is enhanced with a click handler but lacks a keyboard handler for `keydown` events. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot activate the mobile navigation menu using the Enter or Space keys.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing role and tabindex
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module's trigger button (`_trigger`) is selected using a class selector (`.nav-toggle`) but the element may not be a native `<button>`. If it is a `<div>` or `<span>`, it lacks the required `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"` attributes for keyboard accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Content injected via innerHTML without adjacent ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `highlightText` function in the `stringUtilities` module injects `<mark>` elements into text content using `innerHTML` but does not update an adjacent ARIA live region to announce the highlighted matches to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: setTimeout changing visible content without pause/stop control
issuesFound.explanation: The `show` function in the `toastManager` module uses `setTimeout` to auto-dismiss toast notifications after a fixed duration but does not provide a user-controllable pause or stop mechanism. Users who need more time to read the notification may miss the content.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module's trigger button (`_trigger`) is selected using a class selector (`.nav-toggle`) but may not have an accessible name. If the button contains only an icon (e.g., hamburger menu), screen readers will announce it as "button" without context.

Non-native interactive element missing role and tabindex
Content injected via innerHTML without adjacent ARIA live region
setTimeout changing visible content without pause/stop control
Missing accessible name for button

issuesFound.title: Icon-only interactive element missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: Form field missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The search input in SearchInput has an aria-label="Search" but the label element is visually hidden and does not use htmlFor to associate with the input, which is not a robust pattern. The input should have a proper label association.

Line 1245: `<TextInput id={id} type="search" value={value} onChange={(ev) => onChange(ev.target.value)} placeholder={placeholder} autoComplete="off" aria-label="Search" className="site-search-form__input" />`
  Fix: (Also ensure the label uses htmlFor={id} and is not visually hidden if it should be visible.)

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component in ProductCard does not receive an aria-invalid prop when invalid, so screen reader users cannot be informed of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: (This is a component usage issue; the TextInput component itself is correct, but the usage in ProductCard does not show validation. No fix needed here as no validation is shown in the code.)

issuesFound.title: Interactive state not communicated to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The FilterTabs buttons use a visual active state (filter-tab-btn--active) but do not use aria-pressed or aria-current to communicate the active filter to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple :focus-visible rules set outline: none without defining a visible alternative (such as box-shadow, border, or outline-color). This removes the keyboard focus indicator for interactive elements, making navigation impossible for keyboard-only users (WCAG 2.4.7).

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only and .not-sr-only classes use position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which leaves content visible and in the layout flow instead of hiding it visually. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only functionality (WCAG 1.3.2).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 366: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }
  Line 389: .not-sr-only { position: static; width: auto; height: auto; padding: 0; margin: 0; overflow: visible; clip: auto; white-space: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px (SC 2.5.8). This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 490: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px (SC 2.5.8). Icon buttons require sufficient area for touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 499: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is explicitly sized to 20px × 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px (SC 2.5.8). This makes it difficult to toggle for touch users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1092: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em. Negative spacing reduces character and word separation, impairing readability for users with cognitive or visual disabilities (WCAG 1.4.12).
issuesFound.fix: Line 256: body { word-spacing: 0em; letter-spacing: 0em; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Several elements (.skip-link, .btn, .hero-ctas .btn-primary, .hero-image img) use transition: transform without a functional @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable the motion. This can cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders (WCAG 2.3.3).

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the account navigation, footer navigation, and social links have text-decoration: none and rely solely on colour change for hover states. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 as colour is not a sufficient visual differentiator for users with colour vision deficiencies.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which leaves content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it available to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of screen-reader-only text and exposes UI clutter to sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without defining a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus indicator globally for all interactive elements that do not have a more specific focus rule, making keyboard navigation invisible.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible focus ring (box-shadow or border). Users navigating by keyboard will not see which button is focused, violating WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on the mobile menu toggle.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without defining a replacement style. Keyboard users cannot see focus on these links.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot identify the active filter button.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on billing toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: FAQ button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on accordion headers.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on footer links.

issuesFound.title: Social link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on social media links.

issuesFound.title: Form input focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0, potentially overriding the box-shadow defined on :focus. This risks removing the visible focus indicator for form inputs.

issuesFound.title: Reduced motion media query is empty
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty. It does not disable transitions or animations defined elsewhere (e.g., on .btn, .skip-link), which may cause discomfort for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Hero image motion override is empty
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img exists but is empty. It fails to disable the transform transition defined at line 715, causing motion for users who requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making text harder to read for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 253: word-spacing: 0;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle CSS classes and scroll locking but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users are not informed whether the navigation menu is currently open or closed.

Line 1703: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line 1733: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions lack focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, focus is not moved into the drawer (_drawer), trapping keyboard users on the trigger. When closed, focus is not returned to the trigger (_trigger), leaving keyboard users lost in the page body.

Line 1703: const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab sync does not update aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but fail to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the `_trigger` button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is currently open or closed when interacting with the toggle button.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1676: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1706: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` functions in the `dropdownMenu` module show/hide the panel but do not update `aria-expanded` on the `inst.trigger`. Additionally, the `register()` function does not initialize `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger, leaving the initial state ambiguous for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not manage aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The `siteSearch` module implements a combobox pattern but the `mount()`, `_open()`, and `_close()` functions fail to set or update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the search input. This prevents screen readers from announcing when the suggestion list is visible or hidden.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid/markValid do not manage aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid()` and `markValid()` helper functions in `srUtils` manage error descriptions via `aria-describedby` but fail to set or remove the `aria-invalid="true"` attribute on the field itself. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 as the invalid state is not programmatically determinable.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2353: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');
  Line 2366: field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateCountBadge()` function in `productFilter` updates the text content of the product count badge dynamically when filters change, but does not announce this change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the number of products has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2893: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing savings message updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateSavingsMessage()` function in the `pricing` module updates the text content of `_savingsMsg` to reflect billing period discounts, but does not use a live region to announce this change. Users relying on screen readers may miss the updated savings information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3308: liveRegion.announce(_savingsMsg.textContent);

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator number updates are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_animate()` function in `statCounterAnimator` continuously updates `el.textContent` to count up numbers, but the element lacks an `aria-live` attribute. Screen readers will not announce the counting animation, only the final value if `aria-label` is set at the end.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3863: el.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions in the mobileNav module toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer and CSS classes on the body, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the _trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed when the menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1673: function open(announceToSR) { ... addClass(_trigger, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }
  Line 1693: function close(announceToSR) { ... removeClass(_trigger, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu instance functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in the dropdownMenu module show or hide the panel but fail to set the aria-expanded attribute on the inst.trigger button. This breaks the disclosure pattern for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close functions show and hide the panel by manipulating the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded or collapsed state of the dropdown to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (_openInstance function): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;`
  Line (_closeInstance function): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the line `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: While the close function correctly sets aria-expanded to false on the trigger button, the open function does not set aria-expanded to true when expanding an FAQ item. This inconsistency prevents screen reader users from being informed when an answer panel becomes visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line (open function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter category tabs missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tab buttons update their visual active state via CSS classes, but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect the current active filter. This prevents assistive technologies from identifying which filter category is currently applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line (_syncTabAriaStates forEach loop): Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` immediately after the line `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic content updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge and empty state message are updated visually, but these dynamic content changes are not announced to screen reader users via a live region. Users are not informed how many products match the selected filters or if the filter returned no results.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside filterAnimation.run callback, after _updateCountBadge): Add `liveRegion.announce(\`Showing ${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''}\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period switch not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price display updates visually but the change is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that prices have changed or what the new billing period is.
issuesFound.fix: Line (end of setPeriod function, after emit): Add `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label.toLowerCase()} billing\`);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, the function associates an error message using aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers may not identify the field as being in an invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (markInvalid function): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the line `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearing
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked as valid, the function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute, potentially leaving it set to "true" from a previous invalid state and causing confusion for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line (end of markValid function): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or alternatively `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer code references a focus trap variable (_trap) and deactivates it in the close function, but the trap is never created in the init function or activated in the open function. When the drawer opens, keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (init function, after _drawer assignment): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`
  Line (open function, after addClass calls): Add `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close functions manage panel visibility and CSS classes but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button to reflect the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2276: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line 2287: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox (role="combobox") with a suggestion listbox, but the open() and close() functions do not toggle aria-expanded to indicate when the listbox is displayed or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3030: Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line 3039: Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) act as toggle buttons where one is active at a time, but the code only toggles CSS classes without setting aria-pressed to indicate the active state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3626: Add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true", preventing screen readers from announcing the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1806: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is cleared/validated as valid, the function removes the error from aria-describedby but does not remove or set aria-invalid="false", leaving stale invalid state on the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1823: Add removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads, violating the requirement that ARIA state attributes must reflect the initial state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2215: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when registered, failing to communicate the initial collapsed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2347: Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the component initializes, failing to indicate that the suggestion listbox is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3136: Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter not announcing filter results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product count changes, the updated count is displayed visually but not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving blind users unaware of the results.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3440: Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing not announcing billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price changes are not announced to screen readers, so users may not realize the prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3618: Add liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing dynamic aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions show and hide the dropdown panel by manipulating the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but they never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the open/closed state. Screen readers will not announce the state change to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2165 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing dynamic aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is opened and closed by toggling CSS classes on the drawer and body, but the aria-expanded attribute on the hamburger trigger button is never updated to reflect the visibility state of the navigation drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850 (in open function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `_open = true;`. Line 1870 (in close function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: After selecting the trigger element in init, the code does not initialize aria-expanded to "false" to indicate the mobile menu is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1900 (in init function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after the line `_initialised = true;` (or after `_trigger` is assigned).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: Although a focus trap variable is declared, the mobileNav module never initializes it with createFocusTrap nor activates it when the menu opens. Keyboard focus is not confined to the navigation drawer, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850 (in open function): Add `if (!_trap) _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` before the existing code, then add `_trap.activate();` after `_lockScroll();`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation focus not returned on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu closes, focus is not programmatically returned to the hamburger trigger button. Users of assistive technologies lose their place in the page structure.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1870 (in close function): Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });` at the end of the function, after `_unlockScroll();` and the emit call.

issuesFound.title: Search combobox input missing dynamic aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox that shows/hides a suggestion listbox, but the aria-expanded attribute is never toggled on the input element to indicate whether the listbox is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2450 (in _open function): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`. Line 2460 (in _close function): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Search combobox input missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When initializing the search component, the input receives role="combobox", aria-haspopup="listbox", and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not set to "false" to indicate the listbox is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500 (in init function, after mounting listbox): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after the line `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) function as toggle buttons indicating the active selection, but the code only toggles CSS classes and does not set aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2750 (in _syncToggleState function): Inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter buttons act as toggle buttons showing the currently active filter, but they only toggle CSS classes without setting the aria-pressed attribute to indicate which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2950 (in _syncTabAriaStates function): Inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation not setting aria-invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the code adds error classes and updates aria-describedby via markInvalid, but it never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid input field. Valid fields also do not have aria-invalid cleared to "false". Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3200 (in _renderResult, else block for invalid): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` before `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`. In the if block for valid: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` before `addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid);`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results change not announced

issuesFound.title: Pricing period switch not announced

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element during initialization. The ARIA state is absent until the first interaction, violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the current state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init() function registers dropdown instances but does not set aria-expanded="false" on trigger elements during initialization. The ARIA state is absent until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2375: add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside the register() function after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function expands the FAQ panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent state where opening an item leaves aria-expanded incorrect.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3855: add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function updates CSS classes on period buttons but never sets aria-pressed to indicate which period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3510: add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: form validation does not set aria-invalid on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult() function in inlineValidator adds error classes and error messages but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid inputs. Screen reader users are not notified that the field has an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4805: add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`

issuesFound.title: form validation does not clear aria-invalid on valid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult() function removes error styling when a field becomes valid but never sets aria-invalid="false" or removes the attribute. The invalid state persists incorrectly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4802: add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` after `removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function only adds aria-describedby to link the error message but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1970: add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error message reference but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. The field may remain incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1980: add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` at the start of the function body.

fpTitles: form validation does not clear aria-invalid on valid inputs

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links an error message to a field via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot announce that the field has a validation error.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error message reference but never sets aria-invalid="false" or removes the attribute. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions toggle CSS classes and emit events but never set aria-expanded on the navigation trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state of the mobile menu, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

Line 1440: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `_open = true;`
       Line 1458: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The init() function sets up event listeners but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element at startup. The initial state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1519: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the function ensures aria-haspopup and aria-controls are set but never initializes aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial collapsed state is not exposed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1693: add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after the aria-controls assignment.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function animates the panel and updates internal state but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent state where items appear closed to screen readers even when open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2486: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot announce that the field has a validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 963: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error description but never clears aria-invalid. Fields remain marked as invalid even after corrections are made.
issuesFound.fix: Line 972: add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` at the start of the function body.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function toggles CSS classes but never updates aria-checked on the element with role="switch". Screen readers cannot announce the switch state. Note: role="switch" uses aria-checked, not aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2738: add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));` inside the `if (_toggleEl && _toggleTrack)` block.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component receives an htmlFor prop but does not apply it to the label element. Without htmlFor, the label is not programmatically associated with its corresponding input, so screen reader users cannot click the label to focus the input, and the label-input relationship is not conveyed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~770: `<label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing aria-label prop
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton without passing the required aria-label prop. The LABELS constant is defined but never used. Icon-only buttons must have an accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~920: `<IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} aria-label={LABELS[theme]} className="theme-toggle">{ICONS[theme]}</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing aria-label prop
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for closing the mobile navigation does not pass the required aria-label prop. Icon-only buttons must have an accessible name to convey their purpose to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~960: `<IconButton onClick={onClose} aria-label="Close navigation menu" className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div, but role="dialog" is on the inner modal-dialog div. The aria-modal attribute must be on the same element as role="dialog" to properly communicate the modal state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2310: `<div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>`
  Fix: Line ~2315: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>`

issuesFound.fix: Line ~680: `<nav aria-label="Skip links">`
